reasoning: The spectrum shows a single dominant peak at 1369 cm⁻¹ with high prominence (0.97) and a FWHM of 29 cm⁻¹. This is characteristic of the E₂g in-plane phonon mode of hexagonal boron nitride (hBN), which typically appears near 1366–1370 cm⁻¹ in bulk crystals. The absence of other significant peaks and the sharpness of this mode are consistent with high-quality crystalline hBN. The peak position at 1369 cm⁻¹ rather than a blue-shifted value (~1370–1374 cm⁻¹) suggests bulk rather than few-layer material. The moderate FWHM of 29 cm⁻¹ supports good crystallinity without being excessively broadened as would occur in amorphous BN.
